behaviors.description: Log entries with the same trace ID are grouped together regardless of which microservice emitted them.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: yes — a unit test on only the grouping function can miss failures in parsing/normalization + correlator wiring (e.g., trace_id field mapping differences per service). REAL-INFRA: no OS/network required. MOCK-HIDE: yes — mocking parsed records can hide real schema/adapter mismatches. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: no.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real ingestion + parsing + correlation modules together in-process against a mixed fixture containing logs from multiple service formats. Assert that all records sharing a trace ID end up in one correlated request group, independent of service origin.

behaviors.description: The timeline view orders entries by timestamp, correctly handling clock skew of up to 500ms between services.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: yes — ordering logic depends on timestamp parsing, skew-adjustment config, and timeline assembly across modules. REAL-INFRA: no. MOCK-HIDE: yes — pre-normalized mock timestamps can hide parsing/timezone/skew-boundary bugs. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: no.
behaviors.test_description: Execute parser + correlator + timeline builder together using crafted cross-service logs around the 500ms boundary (e.g., 499ms, 500ms, 501ms skew cases). Assert final timeline order and skew handling behavior match spec.

behaviors.description: A latency spike (p99 above 3x the baseline) is detected and flagged in the anomaly report.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: yes — real failures often occur in interaction between percentile computation, baseline windowing, and report generation modules. REAL-INFRA: no mandatory external dependency. MOCK-HIDE: yes — mocking metric outputs can hide threshold/window bugs. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: no.
behaviors.test_description: Feed synthetic latency distributions through the real aggregation/anomaly/report pipeline in-process: one below threshold and one with p99 > 3x baseline. Assert only the spike case is flagged in the produced anomaly report.

behaviors.description: Malformed JSON log entries are skipped without crashing the pipeline, and the count of skipped entries is reported.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: yes — unit-testing parser exceptions alone can miss pipeline-level resilience and counter propagation. REAL-INFRA: no external OS/network requirement. MOCK-HIDE: yes — mocking parser success paths can hide crash-on-bad-record behavior. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: no.
behaviors.test_description: Run ingestion/parsing/pipeline/report modules together with a mixed stream of valid and malformed JSON lines. Assert process continues, valid records are still processed, and skipped-entry count equals number of malformed inputs.

behaviors.description: The tool handles a burst of 10,000 log entries per second without dropping entries or falling behind.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: yes — in-process mocks miss scheduler, buffering, serialization, and real throughput bottlenecks. REAL-INFRA: yes — requires real process/runtime timing and I/O behavior. MOCK-HIDE: yes — mocked clocks/queues/network would likely pass while production drops/backlogs. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: no.
behaviors.test_description: Launch the real tool as a subprocess with production-like config, drive a sustained 10k entries/sec input burst via real I/O (stdin/socket/file tail), and measure end-to-end processed count and lag over time. Assert zero drops and bounded backlog/latency during and after burst.
